Gaurav Kumar Aggarwal

Co-Founder & Director, Revenue Growth·Gulf Capital Intelligence. -DIFC
LeadSteven Downey
Diagnosed problem high

UBO privacy / anonymity in acquisitions

UBO privacy in acquisitions - same structure FCIM uses to keep beneficial ownership disclosed only to FCIM and the regulator.

Contact

LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/ACwAAAWpUd0B5ZDPK7SFvSPUGDtfJeno8z50AVU
Email: Best guess (unverified): gaurav@gulfintelligencedifc.com verify in Hunter
Other patterns: gaurav.kumaraggarwal@gulfintelligencedifc.com, gkumaraggarwal@gulfintelligencedifc.com

Background

I build investment intelligence platforms, invest private capital, and advise on strategy across four live ventures, all with one objective: turning complexity into cash flow.Most founders run one business. Most investors write cheques and wait. Most consultants advise but never operate. I do all three, simultaneously, because the best strategic advice comes from people who are in the arena, not watching from the stands. That is what a Masters in consulting from Hult International Business School and over a decade of operating across the UAE, UK, KSA, and Asia taught me.Here is what I am building right now:GULF CAPITAL INTELLIGENCE (GCI)A private investment intelligence platform, DIFC-registered (Trade Licence CL11954), built by consultants with 20+ years of real GCC deal experience. GCI produces structured conviction reports across 6 specialist intelligence desks, delivering a clear verdict on every opportunity: PROCEED, PROCEED WITH CONDITIONS, or AVOID. Built for PE funds, VC firms, family offices, banks, and capital allocators evaluating UAE and GCC opportunities.BOOST MY BUSINESS AI INNOVATION LIMITED (DIFC)As Director of AI-Powered Revenue Growth, I design and deploy AI-driven revenue systems for physical-location businesses. In one 6-month period, we took 10 businesses from loss-making to 5X revenue using proprietary AI engines covering demand generation, CRM automation, and executive dashboarding.SGD CONSULT FZESenior Advisor on strategy and business consulting mandates, bringing deep expertise in commercial architecture, market entry, pricing, GTM design, and operational scaling for mid-market and growth-stage companies across the GCC.GET JOBS FASTER AIAn AI-powered career toolkit I founded and scaled across 7+ international markets, serving mid-career and senior professionals with ATS-optimised CV tools, LinkedIn optimisation, and job search strategy.I hold a Masters from Hult International Business School, trained at the London School of Economics and the London Stock Exchange, and I am deeply connected to PE, VC, family office, and HNWI networks across the Gulf and beyond.Follow me for insights on GCC investment intelligence, revenue growth systems, consulting strategy, and the Dubai/GCC business landscape.Not regulated financial advice. Not DFSA licensed. AI-generated screening intelligence only. DIFC CL11954.

FCIM solution

UAE Foundation owning a CMA Private Fund. Only FCIM and CMA know the UBO. Founder retains control via Charter and By-laws.

FCIM evidence

CMA private funds permit UBO disclosure only to FCIM (as fund manager) and the CMA itself. Investor identity is not visible to fund administrators, custodians, banks, or counterparties — a level of UBO confidentiality not available in DIFC or ADGM regimes, and stronger than Cayman exempt funds where Cayman Beneficial Ownership Register applies.

Source: FCIM_Private_Funds.pdf, p.7 · ref pf-002

Warm path

Steven Downey via London Business School alumni and CFA Society UAE.

First step

Approach Gaurav Kumar Aggarwal via Steven Downey’s warm path.

Background

He has significant experience handling commercial arbitration and litigation proceedings in the UAE and the EMEA region, particularly in the construction industry. Email: Best guess (unverified): fahim@saeedmohammedalnaboodah.com verify in Hunter
Other patterns: fahim.jalali@saeedmohammedalnaboodah.com, fjalali@saeedmohammedalnaboodah.com

Background

Globally connected and respected in the travel and tourism industry, from London, UK, I bring over two decades of commercial leadership in the travel space across the Middle East & Asia with senior level exposure on the wider global stage to boot. With a proven track record of turning around and scaling travel businesses across regional and international markets, my career includes strategic transformations and digitization of multi-divisional travel operations. From commercial growth and sales, to marketing, customer retention, PR, operations, and IT, I am a seasoned senior executive with all-round exposure of all elements of a travel business, having held various C-Level positions. I specialize in crafting and executing creative roadmaps that drive sustainable growth, boost EBITDA, and unlock value through both organic and inorganic strategies. This includes strategic partnerships, franchises, mergers & acquisitions, IT integrations, and restructuring initiatives. With over 20 years of commercial exposure within The Emirates Airline Group and deep experience reporting at board level, I’ve built and led high-performing teams in both large corporates and startup environments. My strength lies in strong P&L ownership, strategic vision, and hands-on leadership. I maintain long-standing relationships with the world’s leading global travel management companies (Amex GBT, BCD, CWT, HRG), cultivated and leveraged for network expansion, GSA/PSA appointments, supplier incentive programs, and multi-market revenue generation. Beyond TMCs, I am deeply embedded in the global travel ecosystem—with a trusted network spanning airline, hotels, MICE agencies, DMCs, transportation providers, tourist boards, and travel tech platforms—enabling impactful collaborations and results-driven projects. I’m a mentor, motivator, and people-first leader, passionate about delivering excellence in travel while making a meaningful difference through everything I do.

Contact

LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/ACwAAAMqhLIB7-QJyCUJX2iTyAOU7oUgVH5jSaU
Email: Best guess (unverified): patrick@charlesrussellspeechlys.com verify in Hunter
Other patterns: patrick.gearonfciarb@charlesrussellspeechlys.com, pgearonfciarb@charlesrussellspeechlys.com

Background

Patrick is Head of Charles Russell Speechlys LLP's Middle East practice (conducted from offices in Bahrain, Dubai and Qatar) and Chair of the Firm’s International Relationships Committee. He specialises in all areas of dispute resolution with particular emphasis on banking, intellectual property, insolvency, professional negligence and company disputes. Patrick has conducted litigation and arbitration in Gibraltar, Hong Kong, Italy, France, Ukraine, the USA and in all the GCC states as well as in all the courts within the English jurisdiction. Patrick is a leading member of the firm's International Arbitration Group which won the International Arbitration Team of the Year Award at the Legal Business Awards 2018. Patrick also heads the firm’s Bahrain office which won the Bahrain Law Firm of the Year Award at The Oath Legal Awards 2018, 2022 and 2024 and the firm’s Dubai office which won the U.A.E. Law Firm of the Year Award at the Oath Legal Awards 2022. Patrick is one of the lead partners acting in the administration of Awal Bank B.S.C. which involves litigation and arbitration in Bahrain, Saudi Arabia, Switzerland, the United Kingdom, the USA and the Cayman Islands. The dispute resolution team that Patrick leads has been shortlisted in The Oath Middle East Legal Awards in 2015, 2016, 2017, 2018, 2022 and 2024 for the "Middle East Litigation & Dispute Resolution Team of the Year " award; The team won the Bahrain Dispute Resolution Law Firm of the Year Award at the inaugural Chambers Global Middle East Awards 2019, were shortlisted again in 2023 and won the Award again in 2024. Patrick’s team also won the Banking, Finance and Restructuring Team of the Year Award at the Middle East Legal Awards 2025. Patrick was shortlisted for the Middle East Dispute Resolution Lawyer of the Year Award at the Legal 500 Middle East Legal Awards 2024 and 2025. The team are ranked in tier 1 for dispute resolution in Chamber Global and Legal 500. Patrick is a Fellow of the Chartered Institute of Arbitrators and was awarded a CIArb Diploma in International Arbitration in 2018. He was included in the Legal 500 Middle East Arbitration Powerlist in 2022 and 2023. Specialties: Commercial Dispute Resolution and Intellectual Property Dispute Resolution including mediation, arbitration and litigation

Partner·Al Tamimi & Company
LeadSteven Downey

Profile lookup - no specific FCIM problem auto-detected. Manual review.

Contact

LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/ACwAAAgxKu4Bhimht1m_ydTm2YZu5llwPiGeDFU
Email: Best guess (unverified): sabrina@altamimicompany.com verify in Hunter
Other patterns: sabrina.streetonnesaxena@altamimicompany.com, sstreetonnesaxena@altamimicompany.com

Background

Sabrina is a UK-qualified senior lawyer within the Employment & Incentives practice. She advises clients on contentious and non-contentious employment law matters in the UAE, onshore and within the various free zones, including the Dubai International Financial Centre (DIFC) and Abu Dhabi Global Market (ADGM). In addition to the UAE, Sabrina takes the lead on employment law matters in Oman. Sabrina regularly advises foreign and local companies on all aspects of employment law in the UAE and Oman ranging from recruitment, drafting and advising on employment contracts, company policies and procedures, equity awards and incentive plans, company restructures including mergers and acquisitions, end of service payments, pensions, as well as employment-related litigation. She has successfully represented a number of clients at the DIFC Small Claims Tribunal and at the ADGM Court as well as at the onshore UAE Labour Court. Sabrina has a particular focus on health and safety as well as workplace investigations including allegations relating to discrimination, harassment, misconduct as well as protected disclosures and whistleblowing. She has significant experience in supporting clients undertaking complex and sensitive investigations both internally and as an external independent investigator. In addition, she regularly assists clients with the data protection rights and obligations, the protection of confidential information and the enforcement of post termination restrictions in the UAE and Oman. Sabrina holds a second degree in Pharmacology and is experienced in providing tailored advice to healthcare and pharmaceutical clients. She is a member of the Al Tamimi Healthcare, Hospitality and Financial Services practices and regularly provides training sessions and seminars on employment and immigration issues for clients in the Middle East.

Background

About A Certified Public Accountant with Master In Business Administration (Finance) & Bachelor of Legal Laws with comprehensive knowledge of Finance & Operations provided my professional work experience in the field of Finance, Operations Accounting and Audit. Highlights -Managed financial operations for 7 sectors including academic healthcare, hospitality, retail, media, constructions and technology. -Implemented business strategies to improve EBITDA and designed cost-efficient processes. -Developed financial feasibilities for multi-specialty hospitals, day care hospital, medical centers, laboratories, medical university, and new pharmacy openings and monitored BUDGTED CAPEX with hands on experience in PROJECT MANAGAEMENT. -Automated cash handling and reporting processes, enhancing financial controls and accuracy. -Led the implementation of Oracle ERP, streamlining back-office operations across the group. -Designed and implemented online platforms for Medical University, Healthcare Division, improving customer experience and operational efficiency - Extensive knowledge and experience of group financial consolidations (including international, local and public listed entities) - Management and financial & reporting - Financial planning & analysis including budget control - Preparation of standalone and consolidated financial statements (in accordance with the applicable IFRS & local laws and regulations) - Financial and non-financial risk management and critically reviewing and evaluating financial performance & internal controls - Experience and knowledge of ERP packages and other financial reporting tools Key skills - Strong analytic, technical, presentation & research skills - Thorough knowledge of accounting, finance, costing and internal control systems - Strong knowledge and experience of IFRS, IAS & US GAAP together with a good understanding of the UAE business environment - In-depth understating of Microsoft Office tools such as Excel, Word and PowerPoint - Self-motivated, results oriented and have ability to work well independently and as a team member in high pressure and dead-line driven environment - Excellent inter-personal skills. Effective team player & trainer. Adept at managing people, particularly when the team and client are from diverse cultural backgrounds Conclusion By having extensive Finance and Operations knowledge, have attitude to turnaround loss making business unit to profitable operations.

An accomplished top management professional with a distinguished track record in the fields of Business Transformation and Restructuring, Strategy Development, Organizational Design, Balanced Scorecard Implementation, and Project Management. Successfully managed diverse portfolios and assumed leadership roles in several key areas, specializing in Engineering & Construction, MEP (Mechanical, Electrical, Plumbing), HVAC, Low Current Systems (ELV/AV/ICT), Fire & Life Safety Systems, Refrigeration, Water Management, Facilities Management, Elevators, Drives (VFDs), Pumps, Smart Metering solutions and more. Accomplished in identifying and capitalizing on growth opportunities across geographies, industries, and functions; developing and implementing business plans, new market entry strategies, and operating models; including, incubating new businesses • Comprehensive, senior-level experience in MNCs and government-linked national champions, specializing in business expansion and new product development, with full P&L responsibility • Advise senior leaders on emerging industry trends; proven ability to lead organizations through turnaround, critical startup and growth strategies; effectively spearhead investments and joint ventures; private placement and IPO experience • Skilled in strategic planning, market positioning, key account / stakeholder acquisition and management, product rollout, process reengineering; contract negotiation, licensing and franchising agreements, client-supplier relations, government affairs, and recruiting and managing high-performance teams • Track record of leading development activities of more than $150 billion in major projects across six cities, including driving economic development and tenant attraction strategies, improving end-user experiences, designing infrastructure plans for smart cities, and improving the cost and schedule performance of projects dramatically • Deep experience in mixed-used developments and individual asset classes including residential, offices, retail, hotels, industrial, logistics, exhibition and events, and other niche real estate segments; created and managed the development plan, and established framework, for numerous megaprojects, including preconcept feasibility, capital planning, design, construction, highest or best use analyses for market pricing, leasing and facilities management • Extensive experience as a translator between technology and business leaders, and intercultural business communication; consistently develop prolific cross-sector, multidisciplinary relationships

Other patterns: madhu.madaka@bahwaninternational.com, mmadaka@bahwaninternational.com

Background

I am Madhu Kumar Rao Madaka, a senior business and P&L leader with 15+ years of experience across the Middle East, Africa, and Asia, driving growth, profitability, and operational excellence in automotive, defence, specialised vehicles, industrial equipment, and mobility sectors. Proven track record in multi-country leadership, managing end-to-end P&L, leading market entry and turnaround initiatives, and executing large-scale government, defense, and institutional programs in complex and emerging markets. I bring a balanced leadership approach that integrates commercial strategy, operations, supply chain, and project execution, ensuring sustainable growth and delivery discipline. Experienced in working closely with Boards, OEMs, Ministries, Defense forces, INGOs, financial institutions, and distributor networks. Core strengths include: ▪ General Management & P&L Ownership ▪ Regional & Country Leadership (Africa, GCC, Asia) ▪ Government, Defense & Institutional Programs ▪ Sales, Operations & Supply Chain Integration ▪ Market Entry, Expansion & Turnaround ▪ Distributor & 3S Network Development ▪ Large-Scale Project & Tender Execution Currently open to General Manager, Country Head, Regional Director, and Senior Leadership roles within automotive, defense, mobility, industrial, and infrastructure-driven organizations.

FCIM solution

Distressed and special situations expertise — disciplined underwriting, active engagement around recapitalizations, debt-for-equity, and discounted secondary acquisitions. We unlock value where traditional investors are constrained by scale, process, or risk appetite.

FCIM evidence

Distressed and special situations is a core specialty: disciplined underwriting and active engagement around recapitalizations, debt-for-equity exchanges, and discounted secondary acquisitions. FCIM positions where traditional institutions are constrained by scale, process, or risk appetite — typically in the $5m–$50m ticket range that mid-market situations require.

Source: FCIM_Corporate_Profile_2026.pdf, p.4 · ref ds-001

Background

Intellectual property lawyer with extensive experience in trademarks and copyright in the Middle East region. I advise and assist my clients on the best strategies for the protection, enforcement and commercialization of their intellectual property assets to maximize its value. My experience covers wide range of industries including FMCG, media, auto manufacturing, real estate, electronics, technology, finance and hotels & leisure. I manage the IP Transactions which covers IP audits, IP aspects in major M&As, IPOs, JV transaction as well as other IP commercial transactions including, assignments, licensing, franchising, co-branding and other type of commercial agreements. Member of the International Trademark Association (INTA) and has sat on different committees; previous member of the Middle East Global Advisory Council (MEGAC) and currently a member of the Commercialization of Brands Committee. Active speaker in local and international conferences and seminars.

Background

Dina heads up the business development activities within the firm across all regional and European offices. She specializes in client relationships and has built an exceptional client database for the firm based on her many years’ experience in the UAE. Dina initiates and oversees a multitude of projects and programmes throughout the region and works closely with our legal professionals to ensure the optimal client satisfaction. Within the firm she has developed various lines and channels of business locally as well as regionally. Dina previously worked in DIFC prior to BSA where she developed in-depth experience and knowledge on business setups, rules and regulations within the center. Throughout her time in DIFC and overseeing the inception of Dubai’s Design District (D3), Dina has built invaluable connections and relationships which give her unparalleled access to various government departments, authorities and organizations. Dina boasts 20 years of experience within the financial sector, acting as a conduit of information and collaboration to further connect the firm internationally. Her experience in multi-jurisdictional matters has helped to build strong relationships within various industries particularly in corporate matters.

Trade Finance Manager·Agrocorp International
LeadIbrahim Hemeida
Diagnosed problem low

Profile lookup - no specific FCIM problem auto-detected. Manual review.

Email: Verified: karthick.natarajan@agrocorp.com.sg (Hunter score: 99)

Background

Certified Documentary Credit Specialist(CDCS) with Business Banking and Corporate credit control operations(MNC) experience. Demonstrated trade finance process/product flow for digital solutions. Managed 15+ RMs Trade Finance Portfolios, Worked on Streamlining Trade Finance Modules, Cross sales(FX, WM, CASA etc.) and Handled Trade Service desk of multiple branches in UAE. 200% Sales Target Achiever !! GEM AWARDEE 🏆 Independently handled credit control(risk perspective) and trade finance functions for Egypt under major crises. Motivated for new and challenging deadlines; and easily adaptable for fast moving environments. *Passionate Sportsman🏃‍♂️* Coffee Lover ☕*

FCIM solution

SCA-licensed commodity derivatives platform with direct exchange access (CME, ICE, LME, EEX, SGX). Hedging across energy, metals, agri, freight, environmental products — without client maintaining own clearing relationships. Combined with physical-market insight and supply-chain-linked equity strategies.

FCIM evidence

For trade finance and physical-book operators: hedging programs sized to forward shipment book rather than to year-end position. The mechanism that separates firms which consistently protect gross margin from those that absorb full price variance shipment by shipment.

Source: FCIM_Commodity_Derivative_Solution.pdf, p.7 · ref co-004

Warm path

Ibrahim Hemeida via the Egyptian Business Council Dubai and his Egyptian banking and UBP relationships.

First step

Approach KARTHICK NATARAJAN via Ibrahim Hemeida’s warm path.

Background

Successful completion of MBA in international business from Indian Institute of Foreign Trade - Delhi, India which has acted as a springboard for opportunities in the area of international trade & international business development. Furthermore, a certified Financial Risk Manager (FRM) which equips to understand and control the risks confronted in commodity trading. Also had the opportunity to travel on academic assignments and training to France and Singapore. Goals are to lead a company's international business development activities & expand the current business into identified products or markets. Focus is on trading strategy and business development and have demonstrated capability to build and lead the business vertical. Lastly an entrepreneurial, innovative leader with strong business acumen to create opportunities in destination markets and successfully monetize them.

FCIM solution

SCA-licensed commodity derivatives platform with direct exchange access (CME, ICE, LME, EEX, SGX). Hedging across energy, metals, agri, freight, environmental products — without client maintaining own clearing relationships. Combined with physical-market insight and supply-chain-linked equity strategies.

FCIM evidence

SCA-licensed commodity derivatives platform with direct execution access to CME, CBOT, ICE, LME, EEX, SGX and the Baltic Exchange — covering soft and hard commodities, energy, metals, agri, freight (FFA), and environmental products. The client does not need to maintain its own clearing relationship or exchange membership; FCIM holds those at the platform level.

Source: FCIM_Commodity_Derivative_Solution.pdf, p.5 · ref co-001

Warm path

Saran Sankar via UBS Investment Bank alumni and Indian Business & Professional Council Dubai.

First step

Approach Ankur Dhebri via Saran Sankar’s warm path.

An aspiring CPA, with over 8 years’ of experience in Finance & Accounts, Strategic Planning, Fund Management, Budgeting, Auditing & Taxation. Proficiency in managing financial accounting, receivables & payables management, preparing ledger books, bank reconciliation statements and finalization of accounts. Hands on experience in examining financial reports to assess accuracy, completeness, and conformance to reporting and accounting standards. My experience in Mumbai and Dubai, two extremely fast and dynamic cities, has helped me interact with people from various backgrounds and has helped in my personal development on the whole.

FCIM solution

SCA-licensed commodity derivatives platform with direct exchange access (CME, ICE, LME, EEX, SGX). Hedging across energy, metals, agri, freight, environmental products — without client maintaining own clearing relationships. Combined with physical-market insight and supply-chain-linked equity strategies.

FCIM evidence general — service-level

SCA-licensed commodity derivatives platform with direct execution access to CME, CBOT, ICE, LME, EEX, SGX and the Baltic Exchange — covering soft and hard commodities, energy, metals, agri, freight (FFA), and environmental products. The client does not need to maintain its own clearing relationship or exchange membership; FCIM holds those at the platform level.

Source: FCIM_Commodity_Derivative_Solution.pdf, p.5 · ref co-001

Warm path

Saran Sankar via UBS Investment Bank alumni and Indian Business & Professional Council Dubai.

First step

Approach Vraj P. via Saran Sankar’s warm path.

Background

Akash ChandranMBA | Marketing & Finance | Logistics Coordinator | 8+ Years of ExperienceDynamic and results-driven professional with an MBA in Marketing & Finance, currently working as a Logistics Coordinator in Dubai with 3.5 years of experience in supply chain and logistics operations. Additionally, I bring 5+ years of expertise in marketing in India, specializing in brand management, market analysis, and strategic growth initiatives.Passionate about optimizing business operations, enhancing supply chain efficiency, and leveraging marketing strategies to drive business success. FCIM solution

SCA-licensed commodity derivatives platform with direct exchange access (CME, ICE, LME, EEX, SGX). Hedging across energy, metals, agri, freight, environmental products — without client maintaining own clearing relationships. Combined with physical-market insight and supply-chain-linked equity strategies.

FCIM evidence

For trade finance and physical-book operators: hedging programs sized to forward shipment book rather than to year-end position. The mechanism that separates firms which consistently protect gross margin from those that absorb full price variance shipment by shipment.

Source: FCIM_Commodity_Derivative_Solution.pdf, p.7 · ref co-004

Commodities & Natural Resources

Sean Intiomale

Managing Director & Chief Operating Officer·Motjuan Resources and Commodities
LeadIbrahim Hemeida
Diagnosed problem low

Profile lookup - no specific FCIM problem auto-detected. Manual review.

Inferred from search bucket "DMCC Mid-Tier Physical Commodity Traders". Worth manual scan.

Contact

LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/ACwAAADQpJsBMvJQEtPsvKC3nHN4D1rLDfN6WAw
Email: Best guess (unverified): sean@motjuanresourcesandcommodities.com verify in Hunter
Other patterns: sean.intiomale@motjuanresourcesandcommodities.com, sintiomale@motjuanresourcesandcommodities.com

Background

I’ve spent two decades building bridges between Africa’s $586 billion in critical minerals and the global markets that depend on them, fostering relationships among governments, mining operators, international buyers, and development finance institutions. From the DRC’s copper-cobalt corridors and Zambia’s Copperbelt to South Africa’s logistics hubs and the UAE’s trading floors, I’ve structured deals that power electric vehicles, renewable energy grids, and the technologies defining our future. Group Managing Director & Chief Operating Officer | Motjuan In my role as MD & COO, I am scaling operations to move $5+ billion in strategic minerals annually across four continents by 2028, transforming how the world sources battery metals, precious metals, and industrial raw materials while creating prosperity in producing communities. ​My Approach At Motjuan, we don’t just trade and invest in commodities—we architect integrated supply chains and digital infrastructure that unlock value end to end. • Investment Management: Structuring equity, debt, and hybrid financing for mining and renewables across frontier markets, working with sovereign wealth funds, family offices, and institutional investors seeking ESG-compliant exposure. • Commodity Trading: Managing physical flows of critical minerals such as copper, cobalt, lithium, and gold from mine to end users. Our UAE hub provides access to Asian refiners, European manufacturers, and North American OEMs. • Supply Chain Intelligence: Two decades operating across the DRC, Zambia, and regional markets, providing insight into production capacity, regulatory shifts, and emerging opportunities. ​Track Record Before Motjuan, I spent over a decade at IGECO, building the operating framework for one of the DRC’s leading mining and exploration consultancies. I later founded and scaled Intiomale Group, managing cross-border investments across 15 industries. ​What Sets Me Apart While many commodity professionals operate from financial centers, I’ve built my expertise on the ground across Africa and global markets as a junior mining operator. I understand the whole mining cycle—from artisanal challenges to industrial operations—and the political and regulatory dynamics that determine deal success as supply chains diversify. ​Let’s Connect! I’m interested in conversations with investors seeking exposure to critical minerals, mining operators needing offtake agreements, manufacturers requiring ethical supply chains, and policymakers on sustainable mining and ESG. Please include a brief note with your reason for connecting.

FCIM solution

SCA-licensed commodity derivatives platform with direct exchange access (CME, ICE, LME, EEX, SGX). Hedging across energy, metals, agri, freight, environmental products — without client maintaining own clearing relationships. Combined with physical-market insight and supply-chain-linked equity strategies.

FCIM evidence

For trade finance and physical-book operators: hedging programs sized to forward shipment book rather than to year-end position. The mechanism that separates firms which consistently protect gross margin from those that absorb full price variance shipment by shipment.

Source: FCIM_Commodity_Derivative_Solution.pdf, p.7 · ref co-004

Warm path

Ibrahim Hemeida via Dubai-based African diaspora professional networks and pan-African banking relationships.

Elevated DDPEP / state-linked exposure detected. Run enhanced due diligence before outreach.
First step

Approach Sean Intiomale via Ibrahim Hemeida’s warm path.

Neeru Nair

General Manager Operations·Joseph Group
Diagnosed problem medium

Physical commodity exposure without hedging infrastructure

Physical commodity exposure without exchange-cleared hedging - FCIM SCA-licensed platform gives direct CME/ICE/LME/EEX/SGX access without clearing account.

Contact

LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/ACwAAABdNqoBRJ6jMC7VffPtqUtNEb1-6mpnirc
Email: Verified: neeru.nair@josephgroup.ae (Hunter score: 78)

Background

Senior Global Exports & International Project Management Leader (Head of Exports / International Operations) with a strong track record of delivering complex, multi-country projects and export operations across MENA, Africa, Europe, and Asia. I work at the intersection of export compliance, project delivery, logistics, and international operations, helping organizations execute global programs smoothly and at scale. Currently serving as General Manager – Exports & Marketing at Joseph Group, I lead end-to-end export strategy and execution across 20+ international markets. My role is hands-on and execution-focused, covering trade compliance, regulatory approvals, procurement, logistics coordination, and multi-stakeholder project delivery. I regularly work with complex regulatory environments including SABER, CEPA, MOFAIC, CE standards, and Free Trade Agreements. I have led and delivered high-value international rollouts such as OLA Energy’s rebranding across 14 African countries, and managed export and project execution for global brands including BMW, CUPRA, Hilton, Reliance, Mercedes-Benz, as well as large infrastructure programs like the Riyadh Metro. My focus is always on on-time delivery, cost control, risk management, and building scalable export and delivery frameworks. What differentiates me is my ability to simplify complex export and project environments, align compliance with execution, and build reliable, high-performing teams that deliver consistently in challenging markets. I enjoy working closely with stakeholders, solving practical problems, and turning international expansion plans into predictable results. Open to senior leadership roles in Exports, International Operations, Global Project Management, Trade & Compliance, or Advisory/Consulting, particularly where organizations are focused on global growth, market entry, and execution excellence.

FCIM solution

SCA-licensed commodity derivatives platform with direct exchange access (CME, ICE, LME, EEX, SGX). Hedging across energy, metals, agri, freight, environmental products — without client maintaining own clearing relationships. Combined with physical-market insight and supply-chain-linked equity strategies.

FCIM evidence

Hedging applications across the physical-trading lifecycle: locking in margin between origin sourcing and destination delivery; managing freight risk on FFAs; converting USD/local-currency mismatch into rates derivatives; building structured collars around long-dated supply commitments. All under a single regulated derivative trading agreement with daily P&L reporting and margin monitoring.

Source: FCIM_Commodity_Derivative_Solution.pdf, p.7 · ref co-002

Warm path

Region indeterminate. Route to best-matched FCIM colleague.

First step

Approach Neeru Nair via best-matched FCIM colleague.

Background

As a dynamic professional skilled in contract management and supply chain optimization, I offer a comprehensive background in overseeing contracts and enhancing supply chain efficiency. My expertise lies in seamlessly integrating contractual obligations with supply chain operations to drive cost-effectiveness and operational excellence. In the realm of contract management, I possess a proven track record of drafting, negotiating, and managing contracts while ensuring compliance and risk mitigation. Simultaneously, my supply chain proficiency enables me to optimize procurement, logistics, and vendor management processes for maximum efficiency and cost savings. My strength lies in aligning contractual requirements with supply chain strategies, leveraging analytical insights to streamline processes, reduce lead times, and enhance overall supply chain performance. I excel in building collaborative relationships and liaising effectively between suppliers, internal teams, and stakeholders to drive synchronized efforts and achieve mutual objectives. My goal is to deliver strategic solutions that harmonize contractual commitments with supply chain agility, ultimately contributing to the organization's success.

Background

With a deep passion for logistics, supply chain management, I thrive on simplifying the intricate landscape of global operations. Known as a go-to problem solver, I specialize in streamlining processes, enhancing operational efficiency, and developing strategic solutions that resonate across international markets. Drawing on expertise in project management and business strategy, I have dedicated years to ensuring that goods move swiftly and cost-effectively—whether by air, sea, or land. My experience ranges from managing the steady flow of commodities to unraveling the complexities of operations, all to maintain seamless movement across the supply chain. In navigating some of the most challenging market conditions, I bring both agility and precision. Key Skills (or, as I like to call them, my “superpowers”): • 🚢 Logistics & Freight Forwarding – Ensuring that goods fly, sail, or roll to their destinations on time. • 📊 Supply Chain Optimization – Streamlining operations to boost efficiency and reduce costs. • 💼 Commodities Trading & Risk Management – Managing contract executions with a steady, experienced hand. • 🎯 Business Strategy & Market Analysis – Leveraging data to guide sound decisions and foster growth. • 🔗 Vendor & Stakeholder Relations – Building resilient partnerships that deliver ongoing value. • 📈 Operations & Project Management – Bringing order to complexity and achieving successful project outcomes. I enjoy tackling complex challenges and turning operations into a well-oiled machine. If you’re looking for a fresh perspective on logistics, supply chain, or trading contracts execution, let’s connect and explore how we can elevate your business together!

SCA-licensed commodity derivatives platform with direct execution access to CME, CBOT, ICE, LME, EEX, SGX and the Baltic Exchange — covering soft and hard commodities, energy, metals, agri, freight (FFA), and environmental products. The client does not need to maintain its own clearing relationship or exchange membership; FCIM holds those at the platform level.

Source: FCIM_Commodity_Derivative_Solution.pdf, p.5 · ref co-001

Warm path

Region indeterminate. Route to best-matched FCIM colleague.

First step

Approach Caroline G. via best-matched FCIM colleague.

Background

Objective: To be a key asset to the growth of the organization through efficiency and excellence. Summary: • Self-motivated and focused; comfortable working independently or as part of an integrated team. • Ability to lead as well as follow directions, complete time-sensitive tasks and meet deadlines with minimal supervision in a fast-paced, high stress environment.

FCIM solution

SCA-licensed commodity derivatives platform with direct exchange access (CME, ICE, LME, EEX, SGX). Hedging across energy, metals, agri, freight, environmental products — without client maintaining own clearing relationships. Combined with physical-market insight and supply-chain-linked equity strategies.

SCA-licensed commodity derivatives platform with direct execution access to CME, CBOT, ICE, LME, EEX, SGX and the Baltic Exchange — covering soft and hard commodities, energy, metals, agri, freight (FFA), and environmental products. The client does not need to maintain its own clearing relationship or exchange membership; FCIM holds those at the platform level.

Source: FCIM_Commodity_Derivative_Solution.pdf, p.5 · ref co-001

India

Saran Sankar · 1 prospect
Structuring (Private Funds)

Sam Baisla

Chairman·Indo Gulf Capital Alliance
LeadSaran Sankar
Diagnosed problem low

Profile lookup - no specific FCIM problem auto-detected. Manual review.

Inferred from search bucket "Mid-Market Regional PE & Alternative Investment". Worth manual scan.

Contact

LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/ACwAAAY5oY8BuHf3EKzPiZcvfqjt89TOo95BUZQ
Email: Best guess (unverified): sam@indogulfalliance.com verify in Hunter
Other patterns: sam.baisla@indogulfalliance.com, sbaisla@indogulfalliance.com

Background

I convene top funds, family offices, developers, and advisors across Gulf–India corridors. At IGCA, our growing network now includes 100+ engaged advisors and $100M+ in deployable capital interest, with private gatherings being hosted across Dubai, Riyadh and India. We focus on three core groups: For Developers → Access to verified family offices & wealth managers through curated showcases, private dinners, and capital access programs. For Funds & Family Offices → Vetted deal flow, research insights, and closed-room introductions to new opportunities. For Wealth Advisors → A council that ensures recurring, credible distribution channels across the Gulf–India corridors. ---------------------------------- With 24+ years of entrepreneurial experience, I’ve built 10+ businesses, advised 1,000+ companies, generated $100M+ in revenue, and trained 100k+ leaders across Europe, the Middle East, and India. My communities now reach 800k+ professionals worldwide — a network I channel into IGCA. From a small Indian village and Hindi-medium schooling — unable to speak English at 20 — to convening global capital leaders today, my journey reflects what IGCA stands for: vision, trust, and the power of platforms to transform destinies. 📌 Current Focus: Expanding our family office & advisor council. Hosting curated investor showcases in Dubai, Riyadh, Doha, and India. Partnering with developers seeking Gulf & Indian capital. 👉 If you are a fund, family office, advisor, or developer ready to expand across Gulf–India corridors, I invite you to explore IGCA. Reach out: hello@indogulf.capital sam@sambaisla.com

FCIM solution

CMA-approved private fund formation in 10 business days. Optional UAE Foundation as parent for UBO privacy (only FCIM and CMA know the UBO). 100% asset-class concentration permitted. Direct investor representation on fund board / Investment Committee.

FCIM evidence general — service-level

CMA private fund formation in 10 business days from initial submission. The regulator delivers final approval within that window once the documentation set is complete — substantially faster than DIFC or ADGM equivalents, and meaningfully faster than Cayman or BVI when factoring in the CMA fast-track regime.

Source: FCIM_Private_Funds.pdf, p.6 · ref pf-001

Warm path

Saran Sankar via UBS Investment Bank alumni and Indian Business & Professional Council Dubai.

First step

Approach Sam Baisla via Saran Sankar’s warm path.

Unrouted

I am an international, commercially driven CFO and Business Improvement leader. Background

With over a decade of experience as Executive Chairman at Wamda Capital, I focus on fostering innovation and supporting entrepreneurial ecosystems through venture investments and strategic leadership. Previously, as Co-Founder of Aramex, I contributed to building a globally recognized logistics company, gaining deep expertise in scaling businesses and creating impactful partnerships. My work is driven by a commitment to empowering startups and advancing opportunities in the region. At Wamda Capital, I collaborate with teams to enable sustainable growth and innovation across industries.

CMA-approved private fund formation in 10 business days. Optional UAE Foundation as parent for UBO privacy (only FCIM and CMA know the UBO). 100% asset-class concentration permitted. Direct investor representation on fund board / Investment Committee.

FCIM evidence general — service-level

CMA private fund formation in 10 business days from initial submission. The regulator delivers final approval within that window once the documentation set is complete — substantially faster than DIFC or ADGM equivalents, and meaningfully faster than Cayman or BVI when factoring in the CMA fast-track regime.

Source: FCIM_Private_Funds.pdf, p.6 · ref pf-001

Warm path

Region indeterminate. Route to best-matched FCIM colleague.

First step

Approach Fadi Ghandour via best-matched FCIM colleague.

Background

A trilingual (English, Arabic and Russian) marketing professional with 18 years of experience in marketing and communications across major local and international brands such as OMNIYAT, DIFC, EMAAR, DAMAC and many more. CAREER HIGHLIGHTS OMNIYAT GROUP • Spearheaded the creation and launch of a new brand (BEYOND DEVELOPMENTS) targeting the premium segment within the mass market. This involved developing a comprehensive brand identity from the ground up, including defining brand guidelines and crafting a compelling visual and verbal identity. • Developed all required marketing assets, including the website, app, and social media channels, ensuring a consistent and engaging brand presence across all platforms. • Currently leading a city takeover brand campaign and the brand's first product launch, driving significant awareness and engagement in our target market. Dubai International Financial Center • Lead the development and the launch of DIFC Living - DIFC’s first-ever residential project, marking a significant milestone in the organization's history. • Lead the rebranding exercise of DIFC as a mother brand and developed 2 sub brands “Destination” as well as “Gate Avenue” • Developed and led the roll out plans for brand campaigns • Developed 2022 marketing strategy with budgets • Developed departments KPI’s as well as score cards EMAAR Properties • Developed and lead comprehensive product & destination launch campaigns (ie Mina Rashid, Arabian Ranches 3 and The Valley) • Organized international roadshows across 50+ cities in the world by keeping healthy cost to revenue ratio with an average of 1.5% • Developed various programs to enhance B2B relationship and engagement. • Organized large scale events such as product & destination launch events, annual awards ceremony, destination activations...etc • Managed participation in major local and international property exhibitions such as IPS & LPS DAMAC Properties • Lead major project launches as well as brand communications locally and internationally, including AYKON CITY, AYKON Nine Elms, AKOYA Oxygen, AKOYA by DAMAC, DAMAC Towers by Paramount Hotel & Resorts, DAMAC Tower Hotel & Residences • Conducted seasonal promotions i.e.: DSF & DSS campaigns • Conducted lead generation campaigns through domestic & international roadshows • Managed the participation in major exhibitions & events such as Cityscape and ATM • Developed and executed annual media strategy

Background

A man of vision and dynamism, with the determination to succeed, Thumbay Moideen is the Founder and President of Thumbay Group, a diversified international conglomerate headquartered at DIFC - Dubai, United Arab Emirates (UAE). Starting with the establishment of Thumbay Group in 1997 he has set up business operations in 20 sectors. Today, the Group has activities in Education, Healthcare, Medical Research, Diagnostics, Retail Pharmacy, Health Communications, Retail Optical, Wellness, Nutrition Stores, Hospitality, Real Estate, Publishing, Technology, Media, Events, Medical Tourism, Trading and Marketing & Distribution. In 2017, he has been ranked among the The Top Indian Business Leaders in The Arab World by Forbes Middle East. He has been featured in various lists such as 50 Richest Indians in the GCC, Top 100 Indian Leaders in the UAE, Top 100 Companies making an impact in the Arab World, Top 100 Most Influential Indians in the Middle East etc. He has received several Indian and International awards and has been a speaker at various conferences and seminars held in America, Europe, Middle East and the Indian subcontinent.

Background

I'm Rahul - an AI Product Engineer who builds the systems that run real businesses.At Joseph Group, I'm the sole engineer, responsible for the entire technical layer across operations spanning the UK, Europe, Middle East, Africa, and India. I've built an integrated Project Management Platform from scratch serving six departments, a RAG-based contract review system using OpenAI's API, and an intelligent marketing automation agent powered by Claude's API.I don't just write code, I sit with project managers, installation teams, and QA leads to understand what's broken, then design and ship the solution end to end. Frontend, backend, AI pipelines, automation if it needs building, I build it.What I work with: Python, React, OpenAI API, Claude API, RAG architectures, Figma, and whatever else the problem demands.What drives me: I believe the best AI products come from engineers who understand the business deeply enough to know what to build, not just how to build it. I'm working toward building my own company at the intersection of AI and business operations.Previously graduated from BITS Pilani Dubai. Also run a YouTube channel helping students navigate admissions and campus life.Always open to conversations about AI engineering, SaaS, and building products that solve real problems.

Background

Data & AI Executive with 15+ years of experience driving enterprise transformation across Financial Services, Automotive, Captive Finance, PropTech, Insurance, and Healthcare. I specialize in converting complex data into measurable commercial outcomes, leading the design and deployment of AI-driven solutions spanning machine learning, econometrics, and risk modeling (Residual Values Forecasting, IFRS9/ECL – PD, LGD, EAD, etc.). I have built and scaled high-performing teams across EMEA and LATAM, delivering end-to-end data ecosystems from model development to production (MLOps) and governance, and embedding AI into core business processes. My work across pricing, portfolio risk, and real-time decision systems enables organizations to operate as data-driven, decision-intelligent enterprises at scale.Core Expertise:• AI Strategy & Enterprise Data Transformation• Risk Modeling (RV, IFRS9, Credit Risk, Asset Risk, Portfolio Optimization)• Advanced Analytics (Machine Learning, Econometrics, Statistical Modeling)• Pricing & Residual Value Optimization Engines• Decision Intelligence & Real-Time Scoring Systems• Data Platform Architecture (Cloud, MLOps, Model Governance)• Leadership of High-Impact Data Science & Analytics TeamsCredentials & Affiliations:• Chartered Statistician (CStat), Chartered Scientist (CSci), Professional Statistician (PStat)• Fellow of the American Statistical Association, Royal Statistical Society, and British Council for Science• Certified SAS Advanced ProgrammerPublications & Research:Authored and co-authored internationally cited research presented at leading EU and US conferences, with focus areas including:• Residual Value Forecasting & Pricing Optimization• Recommendation Systems• Mathematical & Statistical ModelingTechnical Stack:• Programming & Analytics: Python, R, SAS, SQL, EViews, etc.• ML & Data Platforms: Azure, Databricks, AWS (end-to-end ML lifecycle, MLOps)• BI & Visualization: Power BI, Tableau, Qlik, SAP Business Objects, IBM Cognos• Quant & Simulation: MATLAB, @RiskInterests:Quantitative Finance | Causal AI | Applied Machine Learning in Real-World Systems

FCIM solution

UAE Foundation owning a CMA Private Fund. Only FCIM and CMA know the UBO. Founder retains control via Charter and By-laws.

FCIM evidence

UAE Foundation as the family-control vehicle: Charter and By-laws set the founder’s decision rights, succession protocol, and distribution policy under federal-law backing. Combined with a CMA private fund underneath, the Foundation governs while the fund executes — separating control from operations.

Source: FCIM_Private_Funds.pdf, p.9 · ref fo-002

Background

I am a commercially astute and driven senior finance professional with a passion to make a difference. I am highly successful in building relationships with senior decision makers, driving operational delivery, providing assurance and control during challenging situations, and the creation of shareholder value. I enjoy leading through others and empowering teams to deliver and drive businesses forward, having created and led high-performing global teams from 20 to 400+ employees. I am energised when I can help others succeed and grow, and I have managed and grown the P&L for several businesses ranging from £50m to £5Bn in revenue. Areas of Expertise • P&L, BS and Cash-Flow Management • Finance Operations Management • Business Partnering • Business/ Finance Transformation • Investor Relations – Banking Partners • Financial Planning & Analysis • Outsourcing Offshore & Near shore • Leadership Development • Staff Development-Promotion

FCIM solution

UAE Foundation owning a CMA Private Fund. Only FCIM and CMA know the UBO. Founder retains control via Charter and By-laws.

FCIM evidence

A UAE Foundation can sit as parent over a CMA private fund, combining founder control via Charter and By-laws with the regulated wrapper of the fund. Founder retains decision rights; the fund administers the underlying assets under CMA supervision; UBO is invisible outside FCIM and the regulator.

Source: FCIM_Private_Funds.pdf, p.8 · ref pf-004

Warm path

Region indeterminate. Route to best-matched FCIM colleague.

First step

Approach Michael Mills via best-matched FCIM colleague.

The regulator delivers final approval within that window once the documentation set is complete — substantially faster than DIFC or ADGM equivalents, and meaningfully faster than Cayman or BVI when factoring in the CMA fast-track regime.

Source: FCIM_Private_Funds.pdf, p.6 · ref pf-001

Warm path

Region indeterminate. Route to best-matched FCIM colleague.

Diagnosed problem low

Profile lookup - no specific FCIM problem auto-detected. Manual review.

Contact

LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/ACwAAAOOmqAB_D-MMQ265q97EiC0-QTuDSskM08
Email: Verified: noura@habtoor.com (Hunter score: 98)

Background

As the Chief Communications Officer at Al Habtoor Group, one of the largest and most diversified conglomerates in the UAE, I oversee the strategic planning, organization, and direction of all communication and public information activities for the Group and its subsidiaries. With over 21 years of experience in the hospitality, real estate, automotive, and education sectors, I have developed and executed effective communication strategies that enhance the Group's reputation, brand, and visibility in the local and international markets. I report directly to the Chairman, and the Vice-Chairman and CEO, and I foster and maintain relationships with key media, partner organizations, international institutions, and government officials. I also manage all print media throughout the Group. Additionally, I serve as the Chief Communications Officer for the Khalaf Ahmad Al Habtoor Foundation, where I create awareness and facilitate opportunities for the Foundation to showcase its work and impact. I am also a board member of the Group's Emiratisation Committee and Philanthropic Activities Committee, where I support the Group's social responsibility and community engagement initiatives.

FCIM solution

UAE Foundation owning a CMA Private Fund. Only FCIM and CMA know the UBO. Founder retains control via Charter and By-laws.

FCIM evidence

A UAE Foundation can sit as parent over a CMA private fund, combining founder control via Charter and By-laws with the regulated wrapper of the fund. Founder retains decision rights; the fund administers the underlying assets under CMA supervision; UBO is invisible outside FCIM and the regulator.

Source: FCIM_Private_Funds.pdf, p.8 · ref pf-004

Warm path

Region indeterminate. Route to best-matched FCIM colleague.

First step

Approach Noura Badawi via best-matched FCIM colleague.

Email: Verified: ibrahim-adedejiadefila@alrostamanigroup.ae (Hunter score: 84)

Background

Hi! Thank you for visiting my profile. Looking over it, you will note that I am a technically astute professional with extensive expertise in optimising IT operations, spearheading cybersecurity initiatives, managing enterprise risks and championing digital innovation. Throughout my career, I have demonstrated expertise in implementing robust security frameworks and security measures to ensure IT governance and enhance organisational efficiency. I have contributed to the success of the organisation by reducing security risks, streamlining incident response, refining business processes, and elevating overall security postures. Reviewing my profile, you will notice that I am also adept at leveraging cutting-edge security technologies, mitigating cyber threats, and supporting organisational defences. As an engaging individual, I am proficient in leading high-performing teams, resolving complex issues, delivering cybersecurity awareness, and securing confidential information. Connect with me today to find out how I will make your mission my mission, to help bring all of your business objectives into focus!

FCIM solution

UAE Foundation owning a CMA Private Fund. Only FCIM and CMA know the UBO. Founder retains control via Charter and By-laws.

FCIM evidence

CMA private funds permit UBO disclosure only to FCIM (as fund manager) and the CMA itself. Investor identity is not visible to fund administrators, custodians, banks, or counterparties — a level of UBO confidentiality not available in DIFC or ADGM regimes, and stronger than Cayman exempt funds where Cayman Beneficial Ownership Register applies.

Source: FCIM_Private_Funds.pdf, p.7 · ref pf-002

Warm path

Region indeterminate. Route to best-matched FCIM colleague.

First step

Approach Ibrahim Adedeji Adefila via best-matched FCIM colleague.

Chief Executive Officer - AWR Mobility & AWR Trading·AW Rostamani Group
Diagnosed problem low

Profile lookup - no specific FCIM problem auto-detected. Manual review.

Inferred from search bucket "Mid-Cap UAE/GCC Family Conglomerates". Worth manual scan.

Contact

LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/ACwAAA5ZT-4B5hvvNvA9tlOR23Q_eNFZ-PjTQ3I
Email: Verified: sanaa.ouahmane@awrostamani.com (Hunter score: 73)

Background

Over her illustrious twenty-year career in the automotive sector, Sanaa Ouahmane has been a driving force of innovation and growth. She embarked on her journey in France with the Hertz Corporation, where her direct involvement in operations and her strategic foresight led to remarkable achievements. Recognized for her expertise, Sanaa was appointed as General Manager at the Qatari Almana Group in 2011. There, she played a pivotal role in elevating Hertz Qatar as a leader in the car rental industry. In 2017, Sanaa’s leadership journey took a significant turn as she was tasked with overseeing Almana Used Cars. Under her guidance, the division saw a substantial rise in profitability, marked by her transformative leadership style. Currently, as the CEO of AWR Mobility and AWR Trading, Sanaa leads a range of innovative initiatives, setting new benchmarks in automotive service excellence. Her strategic brilliance shone brightly with the successful launch of SUBSCRIBE ME, a revolutionary car subscription service that has redefined consumer options with its ease-of-use digital interface and versatile offerings. Sanaa’s role extends to managing a diverse portfolio of AWR divisions, including AWR Trading, Royal Enfield Motorcycles UAE, and Autotrust for Automotive Service Centres. With a dedicated team of over 500 professionals, she has been instrumental in cultivating a culture of excellence and innovation within the organization. Her efforts were recognized with prestigious awards such as the Dubai Quality Group ‘Organizational Culture’ Award at Ideas Arabia 2023 for the innovative ‘I CAN WOW’ initiative, which infused the company with a spirit of innovation and attentiveness. Furthermore, she has been honored as the ‘CEO and Trailblazer in UAE’s Mobility Sector’ and received the ‘Best Innovative Mobility Provider in the UAE’ award at the 2023 International Business Awards.

Background

As a Senior Environment Health Safety Specialist at Khatib & Alami, I bring over 10 years of experience in managing health, safety, and environmental programs across various industries. I have a Master's degree in Safety and Risk Management, a Bachelor's degree in Mechatronics Engineering, and professional certifications in Project Management, Six Sigma, and NEBOSH. My core competencies include evaluating and suggesting risk management measures, conducting daily HSE inspections and incident investigations, monitoring and updating legal and client requirements, collecting and analyzing statistics, and producing monthly safety reports. I have successfully led and managed teams, implemented safety programs, and reduced workplace incidents in complex and challenging projects. I am passionate about ensuring the highest standards of HSE compliance and best practices in every project I am involved in. My goal is to contribute to the sustainable development and social responsibility of Khatib & Alami and its clients.

Background

Finance professional with over 8 years of experience in financial reporting, IFRS compliance, and group accounting across multi-entity organizations. Currently serving as Group Chief Accountant at Joseph Group, overseeing financial operations while leading a finance team of 18 professionals. Proven expertise in financial reporting, consolidated statements, budgeting, forecasting, cost optimization, and ERP implementations (SAP S/4HANA). Experienced in multi-bank management, corporate financing, VAT compliance, internal controls, and external audits. Passionate about strengthening financial governance and delivering operational efficiency across organizations.

FCIM solution

UAE Foundation owning a CMA Private Fund. Only FCIM and CMA know the UBO. Founder retains control via Charter and By-laws.

FCIM evidence

UAE Foundation as the family-control vehicle: Charter and By-laws set the founder’s decision rights, succession protocol, and distribution policy under federal-law backing. Combined with a CMA private fund underneath, the Foundation governs while the fund executes — separating control from operations.

Source: FCIM_Private_Funds.pdf, p.9 · ref fo-002

Warm path

Region indeterminate. Route to best-matched FCIM colleague.

Background

Supply Chain Management / Contracts / Finance / Admin / Corporate Service Function in leading organisation of repute in IT & Automotive Industry  Rapidly adaptable to new technologies/tools/Processes and possess expertise in Ariba, SAP & MS Office tools/ Internal ERP applications.  Ability to mentor, motivate team members to achieve organizational goals.

FCIM solution

UAE Foundation owning a CMA Private Fund. Only FCIM and CMA know the UBO. Founder retains control via Charter and By-laws.

FCIM evidence general — service-level

CMA private fund formation in 10 business days from initial submission. The regulator delivers final approval within that window once the documentation set is complete — substantially faster than DIFC or ADGM equivalents, and meaningfully faster than Cayman or BVI when factoring in the CMA fast-track regime.

Source: FCIM_Private_Funds.pdf, p.6 · ref pf-001

Tarun Khanna

Group CEO·Nexus Insurance Brokers LLC
Diagnosed problem medium

Founder with multiple ventures needing structured holdco

Contact

LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/ACwAAAEoWwYBcRTBLXUzLuzHW3cg6RNiVSxofsE
Email: Best guess (unverified): tarun@nexusinsurancebrokers.com verify in Hunter
Other patterns: tarun.khanna@nexusinsurancebrokers.com, tkhanna@nexusinsurancebrokers.com

FCIM solution

UAE Foundation owning a CMA Private Fund. Only FCIM and CMA know the UBO. Founder retains control via Charter and By-laws.

FCIM evidence

A UAE Foundation can sit as parent over a CMA private fund, combining founder control via Charter and By-laws with the regulated wrapper of the fund. Founder retains decision rights; the fund administers the underlying assets under CMA supervision; UBO is invisible outside FCIM and the regulator.

Source: FCIM_Private_Funds.pdf, p.8 · ref pf-004

Warm path

Region indeterminate. Route to best-matched FCIM colleague.

First step

Approach Tarun Khanna via best-matched FCIM colleague.

FCIM solution

Investment banking and advisory: ECM (IPOs, rights issues), DCM (bonds, sukuk), M&A end-to-end, valuations and feasibility, CMA/ADX/DFM regulatory advisory.

FCIM evidence general — service-level

Regional credit and Gulf-real-estate stress is structurally underbanked: the global credit funds operate at $50m+ tickets and the local banks are unwilling to step into impaired positions. FCIM operates the slice between those two — local-resident, regulated, balance-sheet-capable, and willing to underwrite Gulf paper that global desks size out of.

Source: FCIM_Strategic_Blueprint.pdf, p.8 · ref ds-003

Alaa Saoudi

Chief Operating Officer - Express·Aramex
LeadAmr Fergany
Diagnosed problem low

Profile lookup - no specific FCIM problem auto-detected. Manual review.

Inferred from search bucket "Mid-Cap MENA Listed Corporate Treasury & CFO". Worth manual scan.

Contact

LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/ACwAAAPRMRsBBfnMe5XM2sEpCsibak2fGjqWFrk
Email: Verified: alaa.saoudi@aramex.com (Hunter score: 96)

Background

Alaa Saoudi is the Chief Operating Officer for Aramex Express. With over 23 years of professional experience, Alaa is a seasoned executive in the logistics and transportation industry. Alaa obtained his bachelor’s degree in Industrial Engineering from the University of Jordan in 1998, and then joined Aramex, a leading global provider of comprehensive logistics and transportation solutions, to pursue his professional career in the logistics and transportation industry. He started his career with Aramex in Amman, Jordan as Domestic Product Coordinator, where he was responsible for setting up the product standards and processes, the requirements for the tracking and invoicing system and applying technology to better manage the flow of shipments, and since then, he climbed up the professional ladder within the company and was responsible for a number of managerial roles in the operations team in Jordan, KSA and Bahrain. In 2005, Alaa was appointed as Country Manager for Aramex in KSA, and afterwards, he relocated to Abu Dhabi to manage the company’s operation in the UAE. In 2012 he was appointed as Aramex’s Regional Manager for the Levant, and was responsible for managing the teams in Jordan, Lebanon, Iraq, Syria and Palestine. In this role his mandate included full regional P&L responsibility, overseeing a team of over 800 people, making sure the company’s culture is preserved and cascaded to every member across all markets, and all customer expectations are met and service levels are maintained. As a results of the growth of business especially in the B2C and e-Commerce segments, in 2019, Alaa was appointed Global Director of Ground Operations, reporting to the Group COO and managing a team of over 10,000 across 30 markets. He played a major role in leading the transformation in the ground operations to improve the middle and last mile processes, with enhancing the overall customer journey as the core of all transformation plans. Moreover, Alaa is a trainer at Ruwwad for Development, a non‐profit community empowerment organization, a board member with Injaz Jordan, a non-profit organization for the development of youth, and a member in Jordan Strategy Forum, a leading think tank on Economic development. In addition to his engineering degree, Alaa attended different Management and Leadership Programs at Harvard Business School in USA, American University in Beirut, Said Business School – Oxford University - in UK, and Financial course from MERIC in Dubai - UAE among others.

FCIM solution

Investment banking and advisory: ECM (IPOs, rights issues), DCM (bonds, sukuk), M&A end-to-end, valuations and feasibility, CMA/ADX/DFM regulatory advisory.

FCIM evidence general — service-level

Regional credit and Gulf-real-estate stress is structurally underbanked: the global credit funds operate at $50m+ tickets and the local banks are unwilling to step into impaired positions. FCIM operates the slice between those two — local-resident, regulated, balance-sheet-capable, and willing to underwrite Gulf paper that global desks size out of.

Source: FCIM_Strategic_Blueprint.pdf, p.8 · ref ds-003

Warm path

First step

Email: Verified: ram.jalan@damacgroup.com (Hunter score: 99)

Background

𝗜 𝗽𝗮𝗿𝘁𝗻𝗲𝗿 𝘄𝗶𝘁𝗵 𝗖𝗫𝗢𝘀 𝘁𝗼 𝗯𝗿𝗶𝗱𝗴𝗲 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝘀𝘁𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗲𝗴𝘆-𝗲𝘅𝗲𝗰𝘂𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗴𝗮𝗽, 𝗱𝗲𝗹𝗶𝘃𝗲𝗿𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗿𝗲𝘀𝘂𝗹𝘁𝘀 𝗾𝘂𝗶𝗰𝗸𝗹𝘆 𝘄𝗵𝗶𝗹𝗲 𝗺𝗮𝗶𝗻𝘁𝗮𝗶𝗻𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝘁𝗿𝘂𝘀𝘁, 𝗺𝗼𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁𝘂𝗺, 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗯𝘂𝘀𝗶𝗻𝗲𝘀𝘀 𝘃𝗮𝗹𝘂𝗲. For over 20 years in the GCC and India, I have led enterprise-scale digital transformation at the intersection of Customer Experience (CX/XM), Artificial Intelligence, and operating model design. My “Experience Equation” approach aligns empowered teams with customer-centric strategies to drive sustainable growth, particularly in complex, regulated, or high-expectation environments. Where I add the most value 1. 𝗘𝗻𝘁𝗲𝗿𝗽𝗿𝗶𝘀𝗲 𝗔𝗜 𝗦𝘁𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗲𝗴𝘆: Design and deploy scalable AI playbooks and autonomous workflows (such as Tax Bots, Sales Co-Pilots, and Procurement Bots) to reduce manual effort, enhance compliance, and accelerate time-to-value. 2. 𝗘𝘅𝗽𝗲𝗿𝗶𝗲𝗻𝗰𝗲 𝗠𝗮𝗻𝗮𝗴𝗲𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁 (𝗫𝗠): Integrate social listening, conversation analytics, and customer feedback into operational OKRs, embedding a customer-focused mindset throughout the organization. 3. 𝗗𝗶𝗴𝗶𝘁𝗮𝗹 𝗥𝗲𝘃𝗲𝗻𝘂𝗲 𝗢𝗽𝘁𝗶𝗺𝗶𝘇𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻: Orchestrate advanced MarTech ecosystems (Salesforce, Adobe Experience Platform, CDPs) to achieve faster ROAS breakeven and significant conversion gains. 4. 𝗖𝗵𝗮𝗻𝗴𝗲 𝗟𝗲𝗮𝗱𝗲𝗿𝘀𝗵𝗶𝗽: Establish Innovation Labs and Centres of Excellence (CoEs) to prepare organizations for AI and digital disruption. 5. 𝗖𝗟𝗠 & 𝗟𝗲𝗴𝗮𝗹𝗧𝗲𝗰𝗵: Transform contract lifecycle management into a strategic advantage by shortening deal cycles, strengthening governance, and improving risk visibility with AI-driven solutions. 6. 𝗔𝗜 𝗦𝗸𝗶𝗹𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗴 & 𝗪𝗼𝗿𝗸𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗰𝗲 𝗧𝗿𝗮𝗻𝘀𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗺𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻: Equip organizations to develop AI-ready talent at scale by aligning learning journeys with business priorities and fostering a culture of continuous innovation. Sectors Serve as a trusted advisor to real estate, telecom, BFSI, B2C, IT services, and multi-brand conglomerates across the GCC and India. How I engage Deliver business impact through C-suite advisory, end-to-end program leadership, and executive alignment. I lead digital transformation from strategy to execution, overseeing AI and CX initiatives, embedding governance, and driving measurable outcomes. I facilitate board-level workshops, CX and AI maturity audits, and operating model redesign in partnership with senior stakeholders.

Investment banking and advisory: ECM (IPOs, rights issues), DCM (bonds, sukuk), M&A end-to-end, valuations and feasibility, CMA/ADX/DFM regulatory advisory.

Background

As the Senior Vice President of Hospitality at DAMAC Properties, I lead a division that comprises over 8000 keys across various hospitality assets, including hotels, service apartments, holiday homes and residential units and Facilities Management. With over three decades of experience in the hospitality industry, I have a proven track record of delivering exceptional guest experiences, financial returns and driving operational excellence. My career journey has taken me to various regions, including Asia Pacific, Europe, and the Middle East, where I have Asset managed diverse portfolios of hotel brands such as Marriott, Hilton, IHG, Accor, and Radisson. I have held leadership roles at ONYX Hospitality Group and Cycas Hospitality, where I was responsible for multiple regions and hotel listings. My education background includes an Advanced Diploma of Hospitality from New South Wales Technical and Further Education (TAFE) Australia, and various leadership and sales performance programs from reputable organizations such as Pan Pacific Hotel Group and Marriott International. I am passionate about creating a winning sales culture and leading teams to achieve their full potential.

Over two decades of leadership experience conceiving strategic vision, charting digital roadmaps / frameworks, and building enterprise-wide revenue-generating business models for global blue-chip companies within FMCG, O&G, luxury goods, mining, manufacturing, aviation, defence, real estate, and IT spaces. Distinguished for building / leading teams to manage a portfolio of programs worth multi-million dollars, focused on architecting / executing enterprise-wide transformations through digital innovation, strategic operating model design, and cost optimisation to drive business excellence across the entire value chain. 𝐂𝐚𝐫𝐞𝐞𝐫 𝐇𝐢𝐠𝐡𝐥𝐢𝐠𝐡𝐭𝐬 𝐀𝐫𝐜𝐡𝐢𝐭𝐞𝐜𝐭𝐞𝐝 a digital transformation program for a $15B construction group to redesign 200+ core processes, delivering 30% faster time-to-market and double-digit EBITDA growth from an $2M investment. 𝐄𝐚𝐫𝐧𝐞𝐝 the Business Consulting & Technical Sales Award for innovation-led impact and $36M in annual savings with a projected $200M upside by leveraging AI and Automation at scale.

Background

Faik Raid AlHaddadin is the Vice President at DAMAC Properties, where he leads innovative projects and strategic initiatives in the real estate sector. Previously, as a Senior Projects Director at Emaar, he orchestrated the Downtown Master Plan for nearly six years, successfully delivering elite towers across the Opera District and Downtown. This role honed his expertise in high-rise building execution models and modern delivery strategies. Currently a PhD researcher focusing on sustainable practices during project execution, Faik emphasizes the practical application of real-time models and digital twin technologies as essential tools for digital transformation. His academic and professional background positions him as a leader in fostering sustainability within the construction industry.

FCIM solution

Investment banking and advisory: ECM (IPOs, rights issues), DCM (bonds, sukuk), M&A end-to-end, valuations and feasibility, CMA/ADX/DFM regulatory advisory.

FCIM evidence general — service-level

Regional credit and Gulf-real-estate stress is structurally underbanked: the global credit funds operate at $50m+ tickets and the local banks are unwilling to step into impaired positions. FCIM operates the slice between those two — local-resident, regulated, balance-sheet-capable, and willing to underwrite Gulf paper that global desks size out of.

Source: FCIM_Strategic_Blueprint.pdf, p.8 · ref ds-003

Email: Best guess (unverified): ayan@damacproperties.com verify in Hunter
Other patterns: ayan.gupto@damacproperties.com, agupto@damacproperties.com

Background

I am a strategic business leader focused on transforming organizations through AI, automation, and customer-centric innovation. My career is built on bridging the gap between emerging technology and tangible business outcomes—driving growth, operational excellence, and competitive advantage. My expertise lies in architecting and executing large-scale digital transformation programs. This includes leading AI and Gen AI initiatives (like agentic chatbots with deep search integration), deploying sophisticated MarTech automation stacks (SFMC, MoEngage), and building predictive models that turn data into actionable strategy across Marketing, Sales, CRM, and Collections. A hallmark of my approach is creating industry-first digital ecosystems. For instance, I conceptualized and launched DAMAC 360, a comprehensive mobile platform for over 19,000 agents at a leading $10B global real estate brand. This initiative redefined stakeholder engagement and streamlined the entire customer journey. Beyond technology, I build the foundational frameworks that sustain transformation. This involves strategic sales leadership, enterprise-wide business process reengineering, and the deployment of process excellence architectures that ensure scalability, efficiency, and continuous improvement. I am passionate about leading teams to not only adopt new tools but to fundamentally rethink how they operate—embedding a culture of innovation, data-driven decision-making, and relentless customer focus. Core Domains: · AI & Gen AI Leadership | Strategic Business Transformation · CX Journey Automation & Digital Ecosystem Development · MarTech & Sales Tech Deployment (SFMC, MoEngage, Predictive Analytics) · Enterprise Process Architecture & Business Excellence · Strategic Sales & Operational Leadership Let's connect if you are exploring transformation, AI-driven growth, or building the future of customer experiences.

Investment banking and advisory: ECM (IPOs, rights issues), DCM (bonds, sukuk), M&A end-to-end, valuations and feasibility, CMA/ADX/DFM regulatory advisory.

Other patterns: pragyan.nawalgaria@damacproperties.com, pnawalgaria@damacproperties.com

Background

I love understanding businesses and solving complex problems. Always curious to learn, over the years, have developed a knack for getting to the root cause and designing solutions that last—whether through financial models, strategic planning, or fixing process gaps. With 15+ years in Financial Planning & Analysis, the journey has been about building business excellence through financial control, cost optimisation, forecasting, budgeting, and reporting, while partnering with senior leaders and managing stakeholders across geographies. What makes this journey unique is the blend of experiences—scaling a start-up wearing multiple hats and thriving in a corporate setup. Coming from a humble background, financial planning started the day education was self-financed. From studying under streetlights in Varanasi to walking the high streets of Europe, grit and positive stubbornness have been constant companions. This resilience shaped a career that now spans global roles and strategic impact. Have contributed to global manufacturing entities in Chemicals, Polyethylene, Oil & Gas across Poland, Spain, Portugal, Lithuania, Turkey, and currently part of DAMAC, handling UAE and international businesses worth $3–4B. Delivered savings of $46M, implemented end-to-end financial structures, and led ERP migrations. Also proud to have been nominated as one of 10 future leaders globally for the V-Lead Leadership Programme. Beyond numbers, believe in kindness and collaboration. Passionate about mentoring, leadership development, and inspiring through gratitude. Outside work, a poet who loves travelling and storytelling, committed to making a positive impact.
